name: Wesley Hunt for Congress
subject: Twentieth Anniversary of 9/11
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hunt for Congress
body:
Friend,

Twenty years ago, America was changed by the worst terror attack we’ve ever seen on American soil; resulting in the death of nearly 3,000 Americans.

[](http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/u/click?_t=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_m=34dfc555a63d4c7b90db0d0d52ae49c6&_e=X8b6cLIt6M2wVvOoxNM0NQP8HpNgwWqfisNPcKyIn_RA6uFts559mob1l5vtvrVbt2sXb53qAa62O-ayDtx4A864cYQqjokSV2H39NnDlOrmO5rxfJfQ2Ii6ng2q_7kLi67ISHxgopF8jpHpYRZHrQKSOSG1JpLBsOdvpVjL0hz8jHvK_S97T26yDO9HHg4T5gqY3lwzCjoAiMQ8QEz-dixcJU7TSLPUGIQzA2k98RLeC0AxNhaThcPpHi_90gicyLfcud97N9-8dOKk5-3otg%3D%3D)
[Click Here to Watch _Twenty Years_](http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/u/click?_t=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_m=34dfc555a63d4c7b90db0d0d52ae49c6&_e=X8b6cLIt6M2wVvOoxNM0NQP8HpNgwWqfisNPcKyIn_RA6uFts559mob1l5vtvrVbSPvaznqujDWUtIfi3bAMxrjx177HalWirq-kxUZEggyictyakJ_EEH4IDKCTCjHsk9g-Gi7CfNa5yG3bk2Jd68nfgIpAy-v8_yA33MDqeSnyNVH4Dz5MpxE5JeZb_ANYuW2A8YSSYJAJXErnGOGBWVFlb0r1C2mZ4diiObBF1nt1x-KuM9Rj8dlFxBulWRtaqfAf4QHlyDcaVx9Qw59R0g%3D%3D)

Today, we mourn the souls lost twenty years ago, as well as honor the brave first responders, law enforcement officers, firefighters, and members of the United States military who answered the call on and after September 11, 2001.

It is because of these heroes and their sacrifices, that America has remained safe for the past two decades. It has been one of the greatest honors of my life to fight alongside some of these brave men and women in uniform, who committed to a cause greater than themselves.

Despite Joe Biden’s disastrous withdrawal from Afghanistan, we must never forget the heroes who stepped up to the gravest threat our nation has faced since Pearl Harbor. May God Bless America and may God Bless the patriots who gave their lives to keep our homeland safe.

name: Kat Cammack
subject: 20th anniversary of 9/11
body:
Twenty years ago today, too many American lives were lost to radical terrorism in New York City.
---
| | | | | | [ ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Yf8ID2nw1KkuukliH0EybgvVi9O1qn_IKUqvDz_qCUWlp1KkQbEIr-7txg-FWvePXFObO8ww55qNPkMlUGyd51ChLTswRtF7x22hkUPiCw5hBCJ-0JiDrn85CRCjFqtfkg695ZQrlz4zy4dS9NVqcOItga3lJEZU&c=6qlJ24i8MeZnoZbDQtIcQNfeavFFf846bQ3RvKiTDos4XnuLE0DfcQ==&ch=CUI1NspLiHKW-p1fwq5aNxTDpbumaSWSlPtpHgLESPHdMeBXgFM8DQ==)
---
| Friend,
Twenty years ago today, we lost American lives to radical terrorism. The malicious destruction perpetrated by Al-Qaeda was an inhumane but failed attempt by tyrants to break our American spirit. We will always remember those we have lost and we will always stand for our nation.
---
|
---
| During your day, please take time and remember the first responders and the victims of terrorism from twenty years ago. Despite the grief we feel, we as Americans know that we must never cave to tyrannical fear.
We always stand strong in our convictions and perseverance.
This is a time for Americans to come together in somber remembrance of the tragic attack we all suffered. Never Forget and God Bless.

For Florida, Kat Cammack

name: Tom Nelson
subject: If we truly want to honor the memory and legacy of 9/11, we must resolve to heed differently as an American people
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nelson for Wisconsin
body:
# [#]I was working in Washington, DC at the time, my office was a few blocks from the White House and just a few miles from the Pentagon. As the Twin Towers fell and the Pentagon smoldered in the wake of the terrorist attacks, people scrambled to get home, contact loved ones, and find safety from the unknown. People were more afraid than angry amidst the confusion.

I am struck by the similarities and differences between 9/11 and COVID-19. In the fall of 2001, people came together to grieve. Wallets and hearts opened for New York’s heroes and victims. Similarly, while there was some skepticism about the virus when it first hit in the spring of 2020, people were largely complying with stay-at-home orders and masking requirements.

By and large the public supported our initial response in Afghanistan, which was sold as a quick operation to defeat al Qaeda and the Taliban. But President Bush took advantage of the anger and that rare faith in government to launch a long-sought war in Iraq.

As they say: history tends to repeat itself. Well we’ve been stuck in a sort of nightmare loop for the last two decades. It is a crying shame our country did not learn from those lessons. In the spring of 2020, I was hopeful that a pandemic would be one instance where political divisions might fall away and we would become a collective and unstoppable force to defeat COVID.

We have lost at least 650,000 Americans to this pandemic, an official statistic that might be half of the true number. Everyone of us has been touched by this pandemic in some way or other. If we truly want to honor the memory and legacy of 9/11, we must resolve to heed differently as an American people. We must find the fortitude to cast aside threats from within that divide Americans along partisan fault lines. And worse, those who exploit tragedy to advance political agendas at the expense of the health and well-being of our country.

Tom Nelson
VOLUNTEER » [https://go.nelsonforwi.com/a/pledge-to-volunteer]
Tom Nelson serves as County Executive for Outagamie, one of the worst-hit COVID areas in the nation. Local officials have been leading our country through the pandemic because Donald Trump and Ron Johnson have failed. Tom is running for U.S. Senate because we need real leaders in Washington who will take COVID seriously. And if we can take on a pandemic, we can take on anything. And if you can win in a red county six times, you can win anywhere. If you’d like to unsubscribe, click here. clean_body: [#]I was working in Washington, DC at the time, my office was a few blocks from the White House and just a few miles from the Pentagon. As the Twin Towers fell and the Pentagon smoldered in the wake of the terrorist attacks, people scrambled to get home, contact loved ones, and find safety from the unknown. People were more afraid than angry amidst the confusion. I am struck by the similarities and differences between 9/11 and COVID-19. In the fall of 2001, people came together to grieve. Wallets and hearts opened for New York’s heroes and victims. Similarly, while there was some skepticism about the virus when it first hit in the spring of 2020, people were largely complying with stay-at-home orders and masking requirements. By and large the public supported our initial response in Afghanistan, which was sold as a quick operation to defeat al Qaeda and the Taliban. But President Bush took advantage of the anger and that rare faith in government to launch a long-sought war in Iraq. As they say: history tends to repeat itself. Well we’ve been stuck in a sort of nightmare loop for the last two decades. It is a crying shame our country did not learn from those lessons. In the spring of 2020, I was hopeful that a pandemic would be one instance where political divisions might fall away and we would become a collective and unstoppable force to defeat COVID. We have lost at least 650,000 Americans to this pandemic, an official statistic that might be half of the true number. Everyone of us has been touched by this pandemic in some way or other. If we truly want to honor the memory and legacy of 9/11, we must resolve to heed differently as an American people. We must find the fortitude to cast aside threats from within that divide Americans along partisan fault lines. And worse, those who exploit tragedy to advance political agendas at the expense of the health and well-being of our country. Tom Nelson VOLUNTEER » [] Tom Nelson serves as County Executive for Outagamie, one of the worst-hit COVID areas in the nation. Local officials have been leading our country through the pandemic because Donald Trump and Ron Johnson have failed. Tom is running for U.S. Senate because we need real leaders in Washington who will take COVID seriously. And if we can take on a pandemic, we can take on anything. And if you can win in a red county six times, you can win anywhere. If you’d like to unsubscribe, click here. [] [] [] [] [] Paid for by Nelson for Wisconsin

name: Tim Burchett
subject: Never Forget 🇺🇸
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Burchett for Congress**
body:
TIMtalk will keep you updated on the important issues and goings-on in our campaign, in Tennessee, and in our country. | |
---
**Never Forget 🇺🇸**
It’s been 20 years, but most of us remember exactly where we were and what we were doing when those planes flew into the Twin Towers, when so many innocent lives were lost, and when our national innocence dissolved. The date 9/11 will always live on in our hearts and minds.

For the three local Tennessee families who lost loved ones that day, and for so many others, September 11, 2001, is a story of birthdays that ceased, wedding bells that never rang, and endless memories that were never made. These 2,977 Americans were murdered, and tens of thousands more were injured – just for being American.

Today in particular, but also every day, we remember those lives and we honor them. We dedicate every day and try to live more purposely, in their memory.

We remember not just the number – 2,977 – but the stories that defined that Tuesday and the days and years afterward.

We remember the heroes who rushed to help those in need, many who made the ultimate sacrifice to try to save their fellow Americans.

We remember those who went about the hard work of rebuilding at home and of hunting down the terrorists abroad so that our children and future generations can continue to live freely.

We remember the thousands who felt the call of duty and enlisted, knowing that our country and our sacred mandate is always worth fighting for.

We remember that America is the greatest nation on Earth because of the values enshrined in our Constitution.

We remember that those who hate us simply because we embrace freedom and democracy will never win.

We remember and we mourn even while moving forward, always trying to make our country and the world a better place.

We remember. We will never forget.
|
---
**A Climb To Honor First Responders 🙏**
[](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=d9c3d013c7d54ebd882d7874a98ba99a&_e=3CbZShS62l4wgd0VA8PmR6mUnBf4oTWQiA0MnTX3P9zKs16QmmI5fZq4m-fvHW4hBDN98cpIAC10xxiMD3TiaDCrlbEHk7eEe4Vse07bkQ5Ti6S3_Gyrz7ChedkIHAdoEi0wlJ8iupHXhnOgp4gxO4j9r2CvCDq_BR5KSSGzGdxfKyPLBlsVoUM3tSH9-Fg32O4XTcVQzaYSbcSf0oAD3XzubZn9UBM1_htAMe-lnAwydqeMAXRl1b-eMpsnr8IbxbAH740qk-moVyX4GKnDjD9LqUYvEGmtY2LLC4HR75bJLPROwBuetB4NCOhKjyiN-E4srjx2fBg44FwnKSL4ylw8ODFIjioPeA1m1vDW9JpcE0gmJl_QIS9qWDTee4r3LeuA92VEOvVJ00WBy3FgLA%3D%3D)
(Photo Courtesy of the Associated Press)
Firefighters from across East Tennessee participate in the [Knoxville 9/11 Memorial Stair Climb](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=d9c3d013c7d54ebd882d7874a98ba99a&_e=3CbZShS62l4wgd0VA8PmR6mUnBf4oTWQiA0MnTX3P9zKs16QmmI5fZq4m-fvHW4hBDN98cpIAC10xxiMD3TiaDCrlbEHk7eEe4Vse07bkQ5Ti6S3_Gyrz7ChedkIHAdoEi0wlJ8iupHXhnOgp4gxO4j9r2CvCDq_BR5KSSGzGdxfKyPLBlsVoUM3tSH9-Fg3wwu9cZfr-RZZEWY8J1VMgpT055jjJTJ3zjdpv0RuEi9QfjwIlVfSWoZ3rS6bd6rMmW7NUHHBedAv6C5YO-K9En9IN4FHutgEkAoXu4GTPYkyR1S6xM_SSaW0oNE-Bs0MgRfcS3yd9WsYtbtvFXI8LAAqqYbnykRNM8zW3Q8spbq--l_T8tcgrwg8knskkjbJ3SUbjQ3d83XrvZs4HBiQYA%3D%3D). The annual event is a way for firefighters and other first responders to honor and remember the heroes who lost their lives on 9/11.

**Participants will climb the equivalent of 110 stories of the World Trade Center.** Each person will also wear the badge of a fallen firefighter. Knoxville’s event is one of nearly 50 taking place in cities across the United States. **See below for more local memorial events.**

| Friend,

Almost everyone can remember where they were on September 11th twenty years ago when those planes struck the Twin Towers. The events of that terrible day are no less shocking with the passage of time.

[Aaron Rice, an Iraq war veteran and Purple Heart recipient, offers his thoughts and reflections in this moving article. ](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=062e1509fe&e=5703353baa)

---
|
---
|
Let us pause today and remember those who died.

Let us honor, too, the sacrifices made in the years that followed by the armed forces to keep us safe and free.

God Bless America.

---
| |
---
Warm Regards,

Douglas Carswell
President & CEO

name: Elaine Luria
subject: 20 years ago today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Elaine for Congress
body:
|
---
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35601608/305564340/-2135409409?refcode=lur_template_logo&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDA3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjkzNTViOC0yMTEzLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2S0q8rUCDelpu2y_t8528W7_YNOXe163jqZP1p7KEYQ=)

| | Friends, Today is the 20th anniversary of one of the darkest days in American history -- September 11th, 2001. The senseless acts of violence that took place on this day 20 years ago took 2,996 lives. Every one of those 2,996 people had friends or family or neighbors whose lives were also irrevocably changed. Today, I mourn for the victims, their loved ones, and the first responders affected by the events that took place on 9/11. I’m also reminded of my time in the Navy and how, for me and so many others, 9/11 was a renewed call to service. In the wake of these attacks, Americans displayed grit and resilience, and came together to protect one another. For those of you reading this email who were, and continue to be, affected by the tragedy of 9/11 -- I send my deepest condolences and sympathies. Know that I am thinking of you. Sincerely, Elaine

clean_body: | = | | Friends, Today is the 20th anniversary of one of the darkest days in American history -- September 11th, 2001. The senseless acts of violence that took place on this day 20 years ago took 2,996 lives. Every one of those 2,996 people had friends or family or neighbors whose lives were also irrevocably changed. Today, I mourn for the victims, their loved ones, and the first responders affected by the events that took place on 9/11. I’m also reminded of my time in the Navy and how, for me and so many others, 9/11 was a renewed call to service. In the wake of these attacks, Americans displayed grit and resilience, and came together to protect one another. For those of you reading this email who were, and continue to be, affected by the tragedy of 9/11 -- I send my deepest condolences and sympathies. Know that I am thinking of you. name: Laura Loomer
subject: Rewriting 9/11
body:
Twenty years ago today, you saw the Twin Towers fall.
| | | [](https://libersend.lauraloomerforcongress.com/l/jaHkxHkvgw3mMH3vyc0Hsw/dvyiNl9MFaC2892ZqB763XgQWQ/jdkvpZNWElAim7w6SsU1Nw)
Patriot,

Twenty years ago today, you saw the Twin Towers fall.

You mourned the loss of 2,997 lives that day.

You were there as our nation came together under President Bush to take on Islamic Terrorism.

I was eight years old when the towers fell - **and it made a profound impact on my life**. I still remember exactly where I was twenty years ago today.

Today, the Biden Administration and Leftist Democrats want to rewrite history - and they're doing it.

They have redefined our engagement in Afghanistan as an occupation and fail to mention why we went there to begin with - **because they were harboring al Qaeda**.

Pretty soon, they'll be positioning the Taliban as our ally to coverup their Policy of Weakness in the Middle East.

And this week, I was appalled to see a hijab-wearing employee from Virginia's Department of Education lecturing students to not offend terrorists by calling them "terrorists"!

She also repeated **that "Muslims have no responsibility for 9/11!"**

And get this, Virginia's leadership defended the lecture, saying its purpose is to "provide support for Muslim students who may be subject to bullying around the anniversary of 9/11."

What support are they giving to the children and families of our fallen heroes who gave their lives fighting Islamic Terrorists overseas?

We lost 2,218 American soldiers in Afghanistan and another 76 fighting ISIS.

They died to prevent another 9/11.

**They died to stop the growth of Islamic Terror!**

As Biden's America is slinking away from 9/11 and our shared promise to never allow this to happen again, the world, including the Taliban, is mocking us.

Today, on the 20th Anniversary of 9/11, Afghanistan is holding the inauguration ceremony for its new Islamic government - **a direct slap in the face to all of us.**

You can't make this up!

We have to turn things around, or our nation will be completely lost. It is only a matter of time before we are attacked by Islamic Terrorists in America again, thanks to Joe Biden's failed leadership and massive foreign policy blunders.

[**Help me be part of saving America from the threat of Islamic terrorism by supporting my campaign.**](https://libersend.lauraloomerforcongress.com/l/jaHkxHkvgw3mMH3vyc0Hsw/dvyiNl9MFaC2892ZqB763XgQWQ/jdkvpZNWElAim7w6SsU1Nw)

We need members of Congress who are not afraid to identify and name the Islamic Terrorism threat and help defeat it head on.

I will never forget what Islamic Terrorists did twenty years ago.

I will never forgive those Jihadist animals and those who continue to enable them.

And I will never give an inch to those who want to ignore the global threat of Islamic Terrorism.

Please join me in taking time to remember and pray for all of the Americans who died in 9/11 terrorist attacks, all the fallen service men and women who paid the ultimate price, and past and future victims of Islamic terrorism.

Thanks again for all that you do.

Respectfully,

Laura Loomer

name: Harold Earls
subject: Remembering the heroes we lost
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Earls for Georgia
body:
Today, we are reminded of the ultimate cost of freedom and the sacrifices made
on our frontlines to keep us safe...
<>

Hey Peter - it’s Harold. I know it’s a Saturday, but I had to reach out on
such an important anniversary.

20 years ago today, our nation was shaken to its core when a terror attack
took friends, family members, and neighbors away from us too soon.

As the country waited with bated breath for the devastation to end, police
officers, firefighters, military, and first responders put their lives on the
line to rescue as many people as they could that fateful day. So many of them
lost their own lives in the process...

These American patriots will be in our hearts and prayers forever.

It breaks my heart to see how our law enforcement and armed forces are being
treated today. These brave men and women are being painted as villains instead
of the heroes they are.

Have we forgotten the sacrifices they made just 20 short years ago at Ground
Zero?

Have we forgotten the bloodshed at home and abroad to keep our country safe?

Today, on this most somber day, I challenge us to remember the ultimate cost
of freedom and thank the people on our frontlines who are risking everything to
keep us safe.

Let’s give our heroes the respect they deserve.

In freedom,

Harold

Paid for by Earls for Georgia

824 S Milledge Ave Ste 101, Athens, GA 30605

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com

name: =?utf-8?Q?Amanda=20Makki?=
subject: We Remember 9/11
body:
The September 11th terrorist attacks changed the course of this country forever... |
---
| | [ ](https://amandamakki.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0e08b08a89872d093efe5d84d&id=ebdd0a363c&e=c2296cffff)
---
| | Dear Friend, **The September 11th terrorist attacks changed the course of this country forever.** The shock and heartache of these unspeakable acts of terror were felt in every corner of the country, and to this day it remains one of the greatest tragedies in our history. On this 20th Anniversary of commemoration, may we Never Forget this horror, and may we remember those who lost their lives on 9/11.
---
| [ ](https://amandamakki.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0e08b08a89872d093efe5d84d&id=df3a554f6a&e=c2296cffff)
---
| We remember where we were when this tragedy struck - I remember feeling the call to duty when I went to serve at the Army General Counsel at the Pentagon just weeks after the attack. I hope all of you will join me today in bowing our heads in prayer for the fallen, for their loved ones, and for the brave First Responders who run towards danger in order to keep us safe every day.
---
| Never Forget,
_Amanda Makki_
---

name: Mike Braun
subject: 20 Years Ago Today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mike Braun for Indiana
body:
Mike Braun for Indiana

<>
Fellow American,

My heart is heavy as I write this email.

It is hard to believe two decades have passed since the tragedy of September
11th, 2001.

On this day 20 years ago, we saw each and every citizen of the United States
come together as one. In one of the darkest hours of American history, our
resolve as a nation never shined brighter.

We as a nation must never forget 9/11. We are blessed to live in America, and
we must always remember the sacrifices our fellow Americans have made to uphold
the values of our great country. We have overcome so much and emerged stronger
and mightier.

Today, every American must reflect on this day and remember who we are as a
country: united, standing as one. Together -- we represent freedom and
democracy throughout the world.

So many Americans give up so much to preserve this great democracy. We may
have been shaken on September 11th, 2001, but our will to do good at home and
abroad has ignited a movement worldwide.

We will always remember the victims of 9/11. Your memory will never be
forgotten.
<>

God Bless America,

Mike Braun

Paid for by Mike Braun for Indiana

name: Harmeet Dhillon
subject: "Get vaccinated or starve"
body:
Peter, **The Biden Administration just announced that all businesses with more than 100 employees will be FORCED to require COVID-19 vaccines for all employees.** In other words, Joe Biden is basically saying: _**" Get the**** ****vaccine or**** ****starve. "** _ **_I 'll be_**** _ _****_clear_**** _ _****_with you; The Center_**** _ _****_for_**** _ _****_American Liberty WILL sue the Biden Administration the second he enforces this outrageous, totalitarian mandate._** [**Will you support the Center for American**** ****Liberty**** ****to help us sue the Biden Administration for this completely unconstitutional vaccine order?** ](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305329:JnGDzA22N:m:1:3253819041:B4D1353A8D448DA0704ED9F0E0E506D3:r) This isn't an empty threat. _**This is a PROMISE.** _ And the writing has been on the wall for months. First they locked you out of your houses of worship, schools, and closed small businesses... ...then they forced you to wear masks. Now Joe Biden and the rest of his authoritarian minions are going to stop you from making a living and _**feeding your family if you don 't get the COVID vaccine.** _ They don't care about your right to religious freedom... They don't care about your right to medical privacy... They don't care about your right to determine how you should live your life... The only thing that matters to Joe Biden and the radical Left is that you obey. [**That 's why I'm urgently asking you to help us stop this totalitarian takeover of our lives by sending a tax-deductible gift of $35 or more to the Center for American Liberty today. **](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305329:JnGDzA22N:m:1:3253819041:B4D1353A8D448DA0704ED9F0E0E506D3:r) If we don't stand up HERE and NOW it will never end. But we can't do this without you. My name is Harmeet Dhillon, and I founded the Center for American Liberty to push back against these kinds of threats to our civil liberties. And Biden's UNCONSTITUTIONAL order mandating that employers require COVID-19 vaccination is perhaps his worst offense yet. Forget about the astounding hypocrisy. For years they've shouted, "my body, my choice." But it's clear that they only care about "your body" and "your choice" when it fits their political narrative. And if we don't take immediate action against the Biden Administration's new vaccine mandate - your right to make your own medical decisions... your right to individual freedom will disappear overnight. [**That 's why I'm asking you to help us prepare for one of the most important legal battles of our lives with a gift of $35 or more today.** ](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305329:JnGDzA22N:m:1:3253819041:B4D1353A8D448DA0704ED9F0E0E506D3:r) If we don't fight to keep our freedoms today, they'll be gone tomorrow. Thank you in advance for standing with us and for liberty. Sincerely, Harmeet K. name: Mike Gallagher
subject: I’ll never forget
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mike Gallagher for Wisconsin
body:
20 years ago today I was a senior in high school when the twin towers fell and
so many lives were lost.

Friend,

20 years ago today, the twin towers fell and so many lives were lost.

Heroic first responders sprinted towards certain death looking for lives to
save.

Courageous civilians dropped everything to bring those around them to safety.

That day was supposed to be America’s darkest hour.

It was designed to bring us to our knees.

And, don’t get me wrong, it shook us to our core.

But out of the ashes, the spirit that unites us as Americans emerged stronger,
more resilient, and more determined to defend freedom than ever before.

The events that took place that day led me to join the Marine Corps and serve
my country. I wanted to defend this nation, but I also wanted to be part of
sending a signal to the world thatyou can’t attack America without facing
devastating consequences.

As a nation, we promised to never forget the 2,977 lives that were lost that
day and the innumerable acts of selfless heroism that outshone the darkness of
evil and terror.

Today, I encourage every American to pause to keep that promise –– and if you
have children or grandchildren who weren’t alive to witness that day, I
encourage you to share with them what you saw and felt on 9/11.

I also want to take a moment to say to my fellow veterans and their families,
that despite what has happened under President Biden in Afghanistan, your
service and sacrifice was not made in vain.

America represents freedom and liberty for all the world –– and for that
reason we will always be the prime target of those who wish to spread evil.

That is why I will never stop fighting to strengthen and secure America, and
I’m proud to have you with me.

We must do everything possible to keep our country not only safe, but also
free.

Thank you for joining me in remembrance,

Mike Gallagher

Paid for by Mike Gallagher for Wisconsin

P.O. BOX 1027 - GREEN BAY, WI 54305

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com

subject: 20 Years Ago… Today🇺🇸
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LAVERNE GORE 4 CONGRESS
body:
Remembering #911... I believe in our country and so should you. We need one another! | |
---
| | | | Remembering 9/11/01

Do you remember this tragic day like it was yesterday... Waking up with conquering the day on your mind, focusing on the positive day ahead, and the many tasks to scratch off of your “List”?

Only for our country to be violated... I remember the confusion, fright, anger, fear of everyone scrambling to locate their loved ones and demanding answers. On this day 20 years later... the feelings of gratitude for our first responders and fighters for OUR country prevail. We must continue the fight, however! Many individuals still have immense hatred for our freedom and country. Let us remember this day each day, as we continue to defend and search for answers. We are a strong nation and cannot take our freedoms for granted!

#GodBlessAmerica

Let us remember #911 with a day of solidarity, patriotism, and dedication to our nation.

🇺🇸🙏🏾🤝🏾❤

name: Chris Bolling, Terry for VA
subject: tonight’s deadline is something to be proud of
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Terry for Virginia
body:
Chip in before midnight to fund our voter outreach program
| | |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35604965/305575526/-2023814932?refcode=20210911.3_CB_9BP&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzRjOTY3MS0yZTEzLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HPlDYenYNyLLdhRbGzYi1_dH6LJKDUUYq2eFDO3RUSY=)

| | Derek, the finance team just called to let me know that we’ve already hit tonight’s $100,000 fundraising goal.

I was so proud to hear that news — and prouder still that we’re now going the extra mile to reach $150,000 by tonight.

Here’s what hitting this goal means for us:
* More field organizers knocking more doors in every corner of the Commonwealth.
* More flyers and ads directing people to information about how, when, and where they can cast their ballots during early vote and on Election Day.
* More essentials like yard signs, clipboards, and pens. Basically anything you can think of that helps our organizing team!

We all know that in a democracy, when everyone votes, we’re all better off for it. That’s why our voter outreach efforts are so important, Derek. Virginia keeps breaking turnout records and in 2021 it’s critical we do it again.

clean_body: Chip in before midnight to fund our voter outreach program | | | = | | Derek, the finance team just called to let me know that we’ve already hit tonight’s $100,000 fundraising goal. I was so proud to hear that news — and prouder still that we’re now going the extra mile to reach $150,000 by tonight. Here’s what hitting this goal means for us: More field organizers knocking more doors in every corner of the Commonwealth. More flyers and ads directing people to information about how, when, and where they can cast their ballots during early vote and on Election Day. More essentials like yard signs, clipboards, and pens. Basically anything you can think of that helps our organizing team! We all know that in a democracy, when everyone votes, we’re all better off for it. That’s why our voter outreach efforts are so important, Derek. Virginia keeps breaking turnout records and in 2021 it’s critical we do it again. = | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Thank you, Chris Bolling =** Terry McAuliffe for Virginia PO Box 26112 Arlington, VA 22215 If you would like to opt-out of future emails, click. Paid for by Terry for Virginia

name: Rep. Malliotakis
subject: May God Bless this Nation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nicole for New York (Federal) Nicole for New York (Federal)
body:
Nicole for New York (Federal) 20 Years, Still Not Forgotten

<>

Friend,

Today is personal to me and so many of the people that I represent. Here in
Staten Island and Brooklyn, everyone has been affected by this terrible day 20
years ago.

But New Yorkers are tough as nails, and when tragedy struck, they came
together in unity. I am asking all Americans to do the same today... To pray and
reflect on the people whose lives were taken on that day, and on those who
tried to save as many lives as possible.

While 20 years may have passed, the memories of the fallen will never be
forgotten. Please join me in mourning and remembrance today. May God bless this
nation.

Thank you,

Nicole
Paid for by Nicole for New York (Federal) Nicole for New York (Federal)
P.O. Box 60487
Staten Island, NY 10306

name: Jim Jordan
subject: No. Vaccine. Mandates.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jim Jordan for Congress
body:
Democrats want to require covid vaccine mandates but let COVID positive illegal immigrants cross the southern border.

| [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305375:6WstBA2Nf:m:1:3253819041:82B287B3ABFE56EFB518A593D51FA4B3:r)
---

Peter,

Last month the radical left began talking about mandating vaccines for individuals working in the United States Capitol, the military, and beyond. **Now, President Biden wants to "compel" the private sector to mandate vaccinations or face fines up to $14,000 PER VIOLATION!**

**Chances are that if you 're reading this email today you already think that the heavy hand of the federal government plays too large a role in your everyday life**. And we'd agree with you. You may not be the target of the first round of vaccine mandates, but eventually the long arm of government will come for those who aren't vaccinated, and that isn't their role.

**We 've lived through 18 months of a pandemic that both ravaged our economy and put us on a path to financial ruin**. Americans have suffered enough from COVID-19 restrictions, mask mandates, and now potential forced vaccinations.

**[What we want to know from you is if you agree or not. Is it time to let Americans decide how best to mitigate their risk of becoming infected with COVID-19, or should we simply accept that one day soon the government may force you to take the jab?](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305375:6WstBA2Nf:m:1:3253819041:82B287B3ABFE56EFB518A593D51FA4B3:r)**

| _**

[WOULD YOU SUPPORT A FEDERAL BAN ON FORCED COVID-19 VACCINATIONS??](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305375:6WstBA2Nf:m:1:3253819041:82B287B3ABFE56EFB518A593D51FA4B3:r)** _

SURVEY REQUEST FOR: dpwillis67@gmail.com

Response Deadline: MIDNIGHT, September 11th

[** WHERE DO YOU STAND ON MANDATORY VACCINATIONS?
LET US KNOW->**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305375:6WstBA2Nf:m:1:3253819041:82B287B3ABFE56EFB518A593D51FA4B3:r)

---

Democrats told us that the pandemic would be "defeated" when enough people were inoculated to reach herd immunity. The thing is, they keep moving the bar on us.

How can we trust a government run by folks who said that if you got vaccinated, you could take your mask off? **Then soon after, back to masks, no matter your vaccine status!**
**Now, if you work for a company with more than 100 employees, the government is going to try to force you to get vaccinated!**

The simple answer is this: we can't trust Democrats in DC to stick to their word.

**Democrats enact mask mandates. Then don 't wear them. **

Democrats restrict social gatherings. Then break their own mandates to host oversized gatherings where virtually no one is masked.

**Democrats want to require covid vaccine mandates but let COVID positive illegal immigrants cross the southern border.**

_Will the hypocrisy ever end?_

**[Let us hear from you today. Would you support a federal ban on forced covid-19 vaccinations?](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305375:6WstBA2Nf:m:1:3253819041:82B287B3ABFE56EFB518A593D51FA4B3:r)**

Thanks for letting us know -

Thank you,
[](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305375:6WstBA2Nf:m:1:3253819041:82B287B3ABFE56EFB518A593D51FA4B3:r)
Jim Jordan

[** WHERE DO YOU STAND ON MANDATORY VACCINATIONS?
LET US KNOW->**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305375:6WstBA2Nf:m:1:3253819041:82B287B3ABFE56EFB518A593D51FA4B3:r)

| Paid for by Jim Jordan for Congress

PO BOX 355
Delaware, OH 43015

name: Mark Takano
subject: Never forget
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mark Takano For Congress P
body:
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35606388/305580151/-2042826227?refcode=header-img&amount=25&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOTY1ZTk0NS0zNTEzLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dgQVU_x7NjbXcoNRbGQq84LYA7LcUhdV39E5bEZ6zCs=)
---
Peter,

Today marks the 20th anniversary of 9/11 -- the date the world stood still and watched as terrorists assaulted our nation.

We lost countless innocent civilians and many more servicemembers as they fought to defend our nation in the aftermath. Today and always, we must honor those who lost their lives and remember the sacrifices our servicemembers made to protect our citizens, our values, and our security.

**We must never forget the pain and loss we faced on this day, 20 years ago.**

9/11 can also teach us a few lessons and give us a bit of hope.

First, while we rightly cement 9/11 into our nation’s history, we must also remember not to brush off acts of domestic terrorism -- such as the terrible events of 1/6.

Today, right-wing domestic terrorism is as much a threat as foreign adversaries -- and ignoring these forces is unacceptable -- we must speak up and take action to keep terrorist events like 1/6 from ever happening again.

Secondly, in the aftermath of 9/11, American patriotism and national unity skyrocketed. Americans came together and supported one another. We rose up from the ashes and fought back.

As we now face a new threat -- COVID-19 -- we must remember to once again protect one another and our nation by taking a few simple steps: get your vaccine, mask up, and stay outdoors or socially distanced when possible.

Together, I know we will persevere and overcome any obstacle more united than ever.

Never forget.

\-- Mark
| | Paid for by Mark Takano For Congress P.O. Box 5214, Riverside, CA 92517

name: Fred Keller
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fred Keller for Congress
body:
A Day that should never be forgotten
---
| | | | | | |
---
| |
---

Fellow American,

20 years ago, our country witnessed one of the most horrendous attacks on our nation.

While millions of Americans suffered, we came together and persevered through that very troubling time in our nation’s history.

May we never forget those who lost their lives on 9/11 and the service members who made the ultimate sacrifice in the years after to protect our great nation from the ugly face of Terrorism.

God Bless America,
Fred Keller (PA-12)

Paid for by Fred Keller for Congress

---
We are now in our FINAL WEEK of the recall against Gavin Newsom. Voting by mail has been going on for over 3 weeks now, and we're approaching the finish line.

When the recall against Newsom began over a year ago, the corporate media and radical Left wrote us off. Now, **we 're just days away from finally holding Newsom accountable** for his overreaching control, his hypocrisy, and his countless failures.

**This final week is the most critical of the whole recall effort.** We're pushing to secure every last ballot in support of the recall that we can, so can pull off a historic defeat of the leftist establishment that has destroyed California.

We need your help to keep our voter outreach operations fully funded so we can secure the votes we need to finish the job of recalling Newsom.

[**Will you stick with us through this FINAL WEEK and help secure the victory we 've been working toward for over a year with a critical contribution of $10, $25, $50 or $100 or more to Larry Elder's ballot committee?**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305435:6zu1tA2pN:m:1:3253819041:560DC830D805633002EA67366D579E84:r)

We're coming up fast on the finish line, and the ONLY way we're going to win is if we don't take our foot off the gas. [**Please send in your critical continued support to help us pull this off and recall Gavin Newsom.**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305435:6zu1tA2pN:m:1:3253819041:560DC830D805633002EA67366D579E84:r)

name: Matt Jacobs
subject: Reflecting on the 20th Anniversary of 9/11
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Matt Jacobs for Congress
body:
We Stand United, as Americans -

Peter,

On this day, let us pause to remember and honor the nearly 3,000 lives lost
when al-Qaeda terrorists struck our nation 20 years ago. My wife Julie and I
pray for all those who perished on September 11, 2001 – and all those whose
lives were forever changed that horrific day.

Today we remember and honor the bravery of the passengers of Flight 93 and the
heroism of our first responders – those who gave their lives that day and those
who’ve suffered in the years since. We also remember and honor the service and
sacrifice of the brave men and women of our armed forces who, over the last two
decades, put themselves in harm’s way on the other side of the world to keep us
safe here at home.

September 11th is a day to remember and honor all of these people.

It is also a day to remember that there is good and evil in this world. That
there are those who seek to harm us. Not because of what we do, but because of
who we are – because of the values and freedoms that make us American. As the
grandson of Holocaust survivors and as a counter-terrorism prosecutor, I’ve
long understood this basic truth – and that America stands firmly on the side
of good.

Finally, September 11th is a day to remember that we are all Americans – that
the values that unite us are more important than the disagreements that divide
us. We are strongest when we are united. So let us unite behind our shared
pride in this country, our love of peace, and our commitment to the freedoms
that America has always stood for.

If we do that, then America will remain what it has always been: that “shining
city upon a hill,” to quote President Reagan – a place where everyone has the
freedom and opportunity to achieve their God-given potential.

By remembering all of these truths, we honor those who lost their lives on
September 11, 2001, and in service to our country in the years since. And we
ensure that the evildoers who attacked us 20 years ago – as well as those who
seek to harm us in the future – will never prevail.

God Bless the United States of America.

Matt Jacobs
Paid for by Matt Jacobs for Congress
Click here to unsubscribe.
<https://link.mattjacobsforcongress.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=efc64eece5bd468cad54456b97abcf7f&_s=412e44539afb400f88a016b8895d79a3&_t=uKHB3AAABKTm2xDue8Z8QrVp39pWTLEYXT5wlrMbnOL6lVs2nD6LeMqdxFlcskPyn5Xa23aYO8ImGEHxqFP6Q1AWS8_vt3jDkqHg36vh4z5HfPPJc85EzTeVkoacf5fCq7npLcX54jJ62ZCVE8chH8cMgMmgf23wsGPxfHsq1RA%3D>

name: RightUSA on behalf of Hillsdale College
subject: In the face of well-financed efforts
body:
[](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305500:6xEV_A2uN:m:1:3253819041:F4C5DA3456D068B6D94ED1BE1872FB76:r) Dear Peter,
Two years ago, _New York_ magazine posed the question: "When Did Everyone Become a Socialist?"
To say "everyone" is a gross exaggeration. But it's clear that socialism has made--and continues to make--inroads in America, especially among the young.
Today, you can help Hillsdale College understand the extent of that support and the amount of opposition to socialism. With your input, we can form the most effective strategy in fighting for the future of liberty in our country.
**Please give your views on the political, social, and cultural forces promoting socialism in our country by completing your National Survey on Socialism using the secure link below:**
**[https://lp.hillsdale.edu/2021-national-survey-on-socialism](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305500:6xEV_A2uN:m:1:3253819041:F4C5DA3456D068B6D94ED1BE1872FB76:r)**
With your response, you will represent your state as part of a major sampling of opinion that can help us more clearly understand mainstream Americans' views--views that will interest policymakers and opinion leaders.
A recent poll showed that 70% of Millennials are either somewhat likely or extremely likely to vote for candidates with socialist positions. We have seen some of this in recent elections.
**Why is this?**
For one thing, too many young Americans have not been taught what socialism really means or the dismal history of its failures wherever it has been tried.
For another, too many are no longer being taught about America's great heritage of liberty and the importance of the Constitution to freedom.
This is because of the "progressive" Left's increasing success in hijacking American education at both the K-12 and college levels.
_The New York Times_ ' "1619 Project" is a prime example of this. The mainstream media praised this effort to subvert the proper teaching of American history, and its lead essayist even won a Pulitzer Prize.
In the face of such well-financed efforts to indoctrinate the young and undermine informed patriotism, **Hillsdale 's educational outreach efforts have never been more urgently needed**.
By responding to the **National Survey on Socialism** , your honest opinions will help us understand the recent rise of socialism and form the most effective strategy to **reach** and **teach** increasing millions of Americans on behalf of liberty.
We hope to hear from friends representing every part of the country--Americans like you, who still revere our founding principles of liberty, as set forth in the Declaration of Independence and underlying the Constitution.
As Winston Churchill said, "Socialism is the philosophy of failure, the creed of ignorance, and the gospel of envy."
If you agree, please represent your state and take the **National Survey on Socialism** today.
**[https://lp.hillsdale.edu/2021-national-survey-on-socialism](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58305500:6xEV_A2uN:m:1:3253819041:F4C5DA3456D068B6D94ED1BE1872FB76:r)**
In the face of recent successes by the Left, we must remember: **it is never too late to preserve and defend liberty through education.**
Warm regards,
Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844
---

name: Mike Waltz
subject: Terrorism will spread like a cancer
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Michael Waltz for Congress [Click here to unsubscribe
body:
Peter, I have never been more concerned for America’s safety.

With the Taliban taking Afghanistan alongside al Qaeda, their reign of terror will spread throughout the world like a cancer, putting Americans in danger around the globe.

But instead of doing anything humanly possible to evacuate every American and defeating these violent, dangerous terrorists, Joe Biden is taking orders from them and bowing his head in defeat.

Thanks to Joe Biden’s failure, America looks weak, incompetent, and reckless on the world stage while terrorists are seen taking control of our military assets and our allies. It's despicable.

We must hold Joe Biden accountable, and that starts with making sure every single American knows just how badly he failed our country.

I put together a short video outlining the crisis Biden has gotten us into, and I need every patriot on my team to take a look.

**Please[watch our video today to stay up to date on Biden’s Afghanistan crisis](http://links.mwaltzforcongress.com/u/click?_t=d89142ed55974e2696402eb6b29e4daf&_m=3c0f4787b3a44634bd173b32b1e08cf5&_e=rR5y9ALz_z08F_zujhTUGl_AWULBwJbD1uIHLyNtChpUz8WMbwJnm4DCRmCzVmW6LBUgBI6wQsQb1bZ7NjNmNk0S98RmqR7TzWGD9v-fT7jSKy_6wejvooda1qSuINz99Z0Ecw36-tGP91yUS1RH_LDzc2ggwv7PjuXBGhctdTHjxECnkCJD_6C1w4Box7pbXwWzDuVtdx5tbobuWwUFayPVOcvI_Y364369Q4X7KTmBzlpUB-gqORrRzhQVZHKC39nYdTYpkK-d-jnijvI2r6r1tvriYZZCVkYw_03mXdbcd4B8jWWZE5zw3eFUMhsTFFzqLVIb8ttN99xum0QG6uGkefYvRRQ2epfRCSBMZAB9ORAIXSmaYxHG1CN7Yye6cD_zqbV_dHjVAzFe7nnacQ%3D%3D), and after you watch, make a contribution to support a team in Congress that will STOP the Taliban and keep Americans safe. ** [**WATCH NOW**](http://links.mwaltzforcongress.com/u/click?_t=d89142ed55974e2696402eb6b29e4daf&_m=3c0f4787b3a44634bd173b32b1e08cf5&_e=rR5y9ALz_z08F_zujhTUGl_AWULBwJbD1uIHLyNtChpUz8WMbwJnm4DCRmCzVmW6LBUgBI6wQsQb1bZ7NjNmNk0S98RmqR7TzWGD9v-fT7jSKy_6wejvooda1qSuINz99Z0Ecw36-tGP91yUS1RH_LDzc2ggwv7PjuXBGhctdTG7MwPJgltKLmdFmVX599P2YaJM1BaN7w5YWJcFc0PhVtlXf75lzcNO6YHyev7eVxSr8BWiONaKxPqubRdhZvPDbT8M21o1vgv-Eur4Hdeg1rfNuZ7XQh-pBI_Go5D9pERw3ogXJiOvnl-3ITCydE4dyrguzf-rjT9qSxhCbjRsEFWDtop8Cm8cz6NRy5sIebwdbAoCXLwCnRWg9jGJZEwBs2c_UTe0uam-QVXlVv7s4g%3D%3D) In Service, Col. With the Taliban taking Afghanistan alongside al Qaeda, their reign of terror will spread throughout the world like a cancer, putting Americans in danger around the globe. But instead of doing anything humanly possible to evacuate every American and defeating these violent, dangerous terrorists, Joe Biden is taking orders from them and bowing his head in defeat. Thanks to Joe Biden’s failure, America looks weak, incompetent, and reckless on the world stage while terrorists are seen taking control of our military assets and our allies. It's despicable. We must hold Joe Biden accountable, and that starts with making sure every single American knows just how badly he failed our country. I put together a short video outlining the crisis Biden has gotten us into, and I need every patriot on my team to take a look. Please, and after you watch, make a contribution to support a team in Congress that will STOP the Taliban and keep Americans safe. In Service, Col. subject: Our commitment to their legacy --
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marcy Kaptur for Congress
body:
Today marks the 20th anniversary of September 11, 2001 |
Marcy Kaptur | |
---
[](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/tTBUZwcBH_2q13Ow12s-jShXXSgkCJPJf9HhHUpqMKhCtoDkrrRQJ1xSK1Mp16XLCbLu6ITHFvdS2mOwrpcf_tIYpXdUTFxVdebp3qALDWHnB3ok8OYpjcJrcF78uCrKs8jbGVKFNuoUW5xJQxnO2EepCVoR8c6s08i78WAe-k8hpIQRVIxZNaeEGZAzyBO_UxZPsCDcoOtjvQbarQ48UuffO_D6SB3tj1nwQcPlyYPCei3NHWOIbpCTFyFU0FWR0DAlofZhGJIKjeuQMWCN8qjLhqrqZGUSRUiznD3yNGXOGdBVBT9gW6tVEeWoAumh1RITH2H5n2DHrjIbDWf7Cw/3f8/rmtngOnTTimfKo4XWj131Q/h0/L_KLXtlHwCRk6c0NwHdnUqPL6ISzyXTpU4M5vcF1N40)

| | Team,

Today, on the 20th anniversary of September 11, 2001, America remembers those precious lives that were lost and the families and friends left behind.

We honor our first responders, police officers, firefighters, fellow civilians, and other heroes who risked their lives to save others twenty years ago today and during the days that followed.

Let us acknowledge our commitment to their legacy by remembering the strength and courage the American people found in the subsequent weeks and years.

**While the pain and horror of that day are emblazoned in the world’s conscience, may the memory of September 11th continue to inspire us to protect the light of freedom from the tyranny of fear and violence.**

America will never forget,

Marcy Kaptur

| Congresswoman Marcy Kaptur is a champion for the Midwest in Congress. She is focused on getting big money out of politics, holding Wall Street accountable, fighting against bad trade deals that harm workers, ensuring access to health care for all, and restoring our Great Lakes.

name: Derek Marshall
subject: Reflecting on 9/11
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Derek Marshall for Congress
body:
Peter,
**I was in Washington, D.C. when we got the news that the twin towers had been attacked. **

I was at one of my first days of class as a freshman in American University. I arrived at my International Politics 101 class, looked outside the window, and saw the smoke rising in the direction of the Pentagon. Just a few minutes later the military came in and evacuated us. At that time we had no idea how bad it was—the wreckage, the chaos, the lives lost. **All we could sense was that something truly terrible had happened. **

More than anything, I remember the world changing. There are some moments in history that divide us from the past, and this was one of those things. **After 9/11, life in America was never the same. **

If there’s one thing I learned from my studies and my time working abroad in Europe, it’s that’s we need to have a much better analysis in foreign relations than we currently do. Too often in the post 9/11 world, our analysis boils down to black and white approaches to counter-terrorism. But foreign policy should be much more than that; it should be nuanced and comprehensive. **I am a firm believer of the power of diplomacy.** It’s one of the reasons why I hope to sit on the Foreign Affairs committee when I replace Jay Obernolte as Congressman for California’s 8th district.

In many ways, America is currently redefining our foreign policy. Since the end of the Trump presidency, President Biden’s administration has had their work cut out for them convincing other world leaders that our policy commitments can be trusted again. We just ended a 20-year war that many questioned at the beginning.**I always believed it was wrong to go into Iraq and Afghanistan and argued adamantly against hawkish foreign policy.** And I have to wonder, 20 years and over $1 trillion later, where are we?

This much I know: the challenges of the 21st century must be met with better solutions than we’ve previously considered. **We must rise to the occasion and tackle worldwide problems with tenacity and courage.** The COVID-19 pandemic has exposed the cracks in our global commitments and reinforced the need for international collaboration. Climate change will require a similar level of cooperation. **And we can be sure that we will need to maintain strong bonds with our allied nations as we strive for a world with less wars and more diplomacy.** 20 years after 9/11, the losses remain great. But I believe that we are more prepared than ever to course correct.

In Solidarity,
**Derek Marshall**

We just ended a 20-year war that many questioned at the beginning.I always believed it was wrong to go into Iraq and Afghanistan and argued adamantly against hawkish foreign policy. And I have to wonder, 20 years and over $1 trillion later, where are we? This much I know: the challenges of the 21st century must be met with better solutions than we’ve previously considered. We must rise to the occasion and tackle worldwide problems with tenacity and courage. The COVID-19 pandemic has exposed the cracks in our global commitments and reinforced the need for international collaboration. Climate change will require a similar level of cooperation. And we can be sure that we will need to maintain strong bonds with our allied nations as we strive for a world with less wars and more diplomacy. 20 years after 9/11, the losses remain great. But I believe that we are more prepared than ever to course correct. In Solidarity, Derek Marshall * = Paid for by Derek Marshall for Congress. P.O. Box 2836, Victorville, CA 92395 • tel:\(760\ 486-2416)

name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: Sorrow and gratitude on the 20th anniversary of 9/11
body:
| |
---
Twenty years ago, today, the world watched in horror as Islamic terrorists deliberately crashed two jumbo jets into the Twin Towers in New York City. We witnessed our fellow Americans, who worked on the floors above the place where the planes entered, stand at the edges of gaping, fiery holes in the building, trying to avoid the intense flames and thick smoke.

Then we watched the unthinkable: human beings choosing to jump roughly one-hundred stories to their deaths, rather than face the raging fire behind them. Fathers, mothers, brothers, sisters, sons, daughters... forced to choose between jumping to their death or burning alive.

As it seemed like it couldn’t possibly get any worse, we suddenly realized that the South Tower was collapsing with people still inside. Horror is really the best way to describe what it felt like to watch this all happen.

Then we found out that another airplane was deliberately crashed into the Pentagon in Washington, D.C. After this, Flight 93 crashed into a field in Shanksville, PA instead of the White House or Capitol, thanks to the raw courage of the passengers on the plane.

And then the second tower collapsed... with yet more people still inside.

2,977 people were killed, including those who died later from illnesses caused by the dust. More than 6,000 were injured. As of August, 2013, 1,140 people were diagnosed with cancer from being near the toxins at Ground Zero.

It was one horror after another.

There were also shining acts of bravery, from the first responders who sacrificed themselves to save so many, to the passengers on Flight 93. The passengers on Flight 93 knew, at some point, that the plane itself was going to be used as a weapon. They made the decision together to do whatever they could to stop the terrorists’ plan to kill and destroy more, to save others even as they knew it meant their own deaths.

We then went to war, and witnessed still more bravery from American service members. Young men and women signed up in droves to defend our nation. Our troops fought for **twenty years** , doing everything we asked of them. Living and working in terrible conditions, never knowing if they would be one of the ones to hit an IED on the road... they fought, many doing multiple tours.

4,491 American service members were killed during the war in Iraq. The number of American service members killed in Afghanistan is listed as 2,448, but it was impossible to find out if that includes the 13 Americans who were killed two weeks ago in the suicide bombing outside of the Kabul airport.

Thousands of U.S. contractors were killed, and tens of thousands of American troops are living with their injuries, disabilities, and trauma.

Finally, there are still Americans who were abandoned in Afghanistan by the Biden administration, including pregnant women. Americans have been left there to fend for themselves against the Taliban and ISIS.

There is a lot I could say about the way in which Joe Biden (or whoever handles him) chose to (mis)manage America’s withdrawal from Afghanistan. The fact that the Taliban is back in control of Afghanistan, and is holding the inauguration of their new government today, on the 20th anniversary of 9/11, the impetus for the war. Joe Biden’s “plan” was, without a doubt, an **unmitigated disaster and entirely preventable had he or anyone in the White House cared about preventing chaos, tragedy, and death.**

However, today I’d rather call attention to an article from The Federalist, “How To Honor The 9/11 Generation Of American Veterans,” by Stephen Roberts. Below is an excerpt, but please [read the whole thing](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/6/?T=NDkxMDk0ODk%3AMDItYjIxMjU0LWNhNTcyNGU4ODIxOTQwMTA4ZjQyMTQ3MDM4ZGUyN2Zj%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR%3AZmFsc2U%3AMA%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly90aGVmZWRlcmFsaXN0LmNvbS8yMDIxLzA5LzA4L2hvdy10by1ob25vci10aGUtOS0xMS1nZW5lcmF0aW9uLW9mLWFtZXJpY2FuLXZldGVyYW5zLyUyMD9taWQ9NDQzMDcmaWQ9VDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR&K=Mn_zLjCSSJ6PytPmm85T3w).

_To compound the woundedness that came from the indifference of the American people, those who served in Afghanistan over the last 20 years are now wondering whether their service meant anything. The post-mortems have not helped, with their relentless focus on perceived failures. During the recent withdrawal from the country, calls to the Veterans Affairs suicide hotline [increased dramatically](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/6/?T=NDkxMDk0ODk%3AMDItYjIxMjU0LWNhNTcyNGU4ODIxOTQwMTA4ZjQyMTQ3MDM4ZGUyN2Zj%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR%3AZmFsc2U%3AMQ%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cud2FzaGluZ3RvbmV4YW1pbmVyLmNvbS9wb2xpY3kvZGVmZW5zZS9uYXRpb25hbC1zZWN1cml0eS92ZXRlcmFucy1hZmZhaXJzLXN1aWNpZGUtaG90bGluZS1hZmdoYW5pc3Rhbj9taWQ9NDQzMDcmaWQ9VDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR&K=66do6i_2YKkE3qTPLm5fUA)._

_Regardless of political affiliations or opinions about the war over there, this is no way to treat the warriors who took up the mantle of prior generations to keep our country safe. _

Roberts continues with suggestions for us all to help and encourage American veterans, and ends with a message to remember what they did for us.

_The war in Afghanistan was anything but meaningless, and the service rendered by our troops over the last 20 years is nothing short of heroic and awe-inspiring. We could learn a lot as a country from the way they stood together, served together, suffered together, and at times bled together._

_Now, we welcome them home. Many of the 18-year-olds who enlisted in the wake of 9/11 are reaching retirement this year. They have seen the best and worst of the world and bring a world of wisdom back to the society they fought for._

_Let’s acknowledge the cost. It’s not just those killed or maimed in battle, but also those who came back with empty hearts, or to empty homes, and have never recovered. As a nation, we must also find veterans on the fringes and bring them back into its heart. We need them as much as they need us._

In our criticism of politicians and military leadership who cared more about Critical Race Theory than their own troops in the middle of a war, we must not forget the American service members who fought bravely and stoically for twenty years. They fought for you, for me, for our children and grandchildren.

So, today, we remember and honor those who lost their lives in the evil terrorist attack on September 11th, 2001, including the heroic first responders from that day. Today, we also remember and honor those who answered the call, and fought in Iraq and Afghanistan, sacrificing so much.

Thank you.

Jenny Beth Martin

[1] [https://thefederalist.com/2021/09/08/how-to-honor-the-9-11-generation-of-american-veterans/](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/6/?T=NDkxMDk0ODk%3AMDItYjIxMjU0LWNhNTcyNGU4ODIxOTQwMTA4ZjQyMTQ3MDM4ZGUyN2Zj%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR%3AZmFsc2U%3AMg%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly90aGVmZWRlcmFsaXN0LmNvbS8yMDIxLzA5LzA4L2hvdy10by1ob25vci10aGUtOS0xMS1nZW5lcmF0aW9uLW9mLWFtZXJpY2FuLXZldGVyYW5zLz9taWQ9NDQzMDcmaWQ9VDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR&K=OMLHlBPZ-3SUjrk0CBX74Q)
[HOST A PROTEST](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/6/?T=NDkxMDk0ODk%3AMDItYjIxMjU0LWNhNTcyNGU4ODIxOTQwMTA4ZjQyMTQ3MDM4ZGUyN2Zj%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR%3AZmFsc2U%3AMw%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGVhcGFydHlwYXRyaW90cy5vcmcvc3RvcG1lZGljYWxtYW5kYXRlcy8_bWlkPTQ0MzA3JmlkPVQxOG85TG5ldWpIaGs5MVFRUQ&K=yTroBeO2kF-WtYo3p7ZPUw)
In liberty,

Jenny Beth Martin
|

name: Bruno Amato
subject: We haven’t forgotten
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bruno Amato for Congress
body:
Today, we honor those we lost and those who put their own lives in danger to save others. | | | [](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDCqDFOot2V74LWBH_4loCzqxLBs6P9rwfiSzPrHJPso6-xqhUC0ONejTOMTvAc__86a0YQOZ19abZJjdvPGEIpTgxUEueaNcflQUoGbl3w1RcjgmdTzL00JoeBXEDKwnNmb6bb2eOB0KkcMyagxE166cSFRXRLE1Q6gsloCpPfejOo4MPeqMyIRZCZsR7GpXJXJKzmgMCcopp8XsCDBjcIt80w8qmxItfRp56cKPkZ7VNYGz1W2hupzjeMjsfbQHOQ/3f8/h1NhX-RDRHuIgQuEz_eEZg/h0/J376QFWe3LsPMv8Pqxkx2EY3y5xRGkK8MMP93vifPko)
---
Folks.

Twenty years ago today, almost three thousand Americans were killed in the worst act of terror our country has ever seen.

While we can never bring back those we lost, we can all look at the heroic actions of everyday Americans on that day to see what our country is truly made of.

The first responders who ran toward chaos and danger to save others, the people of Flight 93 who sacrificed their lives to save hundreds if not thousands on the ground, and the brave members of our military who signed up to serve in the years of conflict that followed -- we all owe these people a deep debt of gratitude.

Today, I will be thinking about all of them as we memorialize these horrible attacks.

Thank you,

Bruno Amato

name: Terry McAuliffe
subject: I wanted to tell you directly before midnight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Terry for Virginia
body:
| | |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35612404/305599151/-2023814932?refcode=20210911.5_TM_9BR&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZDQwN2EzMi01ODEzLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UE-15p8z3UycM_d5L8Vf_P9WbPCV8-7f0y7DaaKo2yE=)

| | Derek,

Before our midnight deadline, I wanted to tell you directly why I’m running to be Virginia’s next Governor.

We are running an historic campaign here in Virginia. Not only are we building the largest grassroots movement in Virginia history, but we’re running a campaign that speaks to the issues that matter most to Virginians.

And that’s exactly why I’m running.

I’m running because every single Virginia child deserves a **quality education** — which is why I will make the largest-ever investment in education in the Commonwealth’s history.

I’m running because every Virginian has a right to **affordable, quality health care** and prescription medications.

I’m running because we need a leader who will champion initiatives to **curb gun violence** in Virginia.

I’m running because it's long past time for innovative strategies to secure our **clean energy future.**

And Derek, I’m running because every single person in our Commonwealth deserves to live in a state where they have opportunities for success.

clean_body: | | | = | | Derek, Before our midnight deadline, I wanted to tell you directly why I’m running to be Virginia’s next Governor. We are running an historic campaign here in Virginia. Not only are we building the largest grassroots movement in Virginia history, but we’re running a campaign that speaks to the issues that matter most to Virginians. And that’s exactly why I’m running. I’m running because every single Virginia child deserves a quality education — which is why I will make the largest-ever investment in education in the Commonwealth’s history. I’m running because every Virginian has a right to affordable, quality health care and prescription medications. I’m running because we need a leader who will champion initiatives to curb gun violence in Virginia. I’m running because it's long past time for innovative strategies to secure our clean energy future. And Derek, I’m running because every single person in our Commonwealth deserves to live in a state where they have opportunities for success. = | | | = ** | = | | = | = | | = | = Thank you for being on this team, Terry McAuliffe =** Terry McAuliffe for Virginia PO Box 26112 Arlington, VA 22215 If you would like to opt-out of future emails, click. name: Culp for Congress
subject: 20 Years Later...Remembering 9/11
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CULP FOR CONGRESS
body:
View online [https://app.getresponse.com/view.html?x=a62b&m=BwK0E1&mc=Jm&s=Bttw6dS&u=Q96gS&z=EJ9Kyzh&]

[https://m.gr-cdn-5.com/getresponse-Q96gS/photos/4ff28e94-9aa7-477b-a28d-6c3eb69de3a9.png]

September 11th, 2001 marks the last and largest foreign attack on United States soil.

Twenty years ago our entire nation was shaken as an airplane flew into one of the Twin Towers in New York City. Initially as we watched we weren't sure of the motive, but as a second plane struck the south tower and reports began to come out of other hijacked planes in our country, it was clear that this was a planned attack.

Both planes had been hijacked by terrorists who were bent on attacking Americans and crippling our country.

On 9/11 our country was wounded. As a nation, we mourned the lives that were taken and the families that would never be together again. The survivors of 9/11 and the people that lost loved ones that day know the sufferings on a brutal and personal level.

But something happened that was unexpected.

An enemy of our nation had tried to bring America to its knees. Instead, we rose up as a nation and our hearts were flooded with patriotism, love of country, and love for our fellow Americans. As Americans we looked to how we could work together, to help each other through this tragedy.

[https://m.gr-cdn-5.com/getresponse-Q96gS/photos/658c7368-d914-4e2b-8610-4d1c7850e28f.jpeg]

Now we’re seeing attacks on our individual freedom and personal choice from elected officials in our own government.

During Biden's speech on his new COVID measures on Sept 9th, 2021 he said, “This is not about freedom or personal choice. It’s about protecting yourself and those around you.” Biden and his administration again have shown their all-out assault on our country and complete disregard for our Constitution.

What gives me hope on the anniversary of 9/11 is that once more there is a swell of patriotism from you, the American people, and a determination to push back against unconstitutional laws and mandates.

"We the people" remember what it felt like to have our country attacked and what we felt as we watched the Twin Towers fall. We'll never forget 9/11 because we love our country and respect its history. We will protect America from all threats, both foreign and domestic.

Stand up for America [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&lc=SKbGpA&mc=Jm&s=Bttw6dS&u=Q96gS&z=Ewju9Uy&]

Loren

I WILL STEP UP AND DO $50! [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&lc=SKbG18&mc=Jm&s=Bttw6dS&u=Q96gS&z=EEAUz3I&]

THIS IS IMPORTANT. I CAN DO $100! [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&lc=SKbG3I&mc=Jm&s=Bttw6dS&u=Q96gS&z=EyK3tvk&]

I CAN PLAY BIG LEAGUE AND DO $250! [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&lc=SKbGN3&mc=Jm&s=Bttw6dS&u=Q96gS&z=EtOjQuH&]

I WANT TO BE ON CULP'S ADVISORY TEAM AND DO $2,900! [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&lc=SKbGdK&mc=Jm&s=Bttw6dS&u=Q96gS&z=EBlCMuN&]

| | Twenty years later our nation still holds a solemn remembrance of this day. We will never forget and will always grieve the loss of life on that terrible morning. And we remain grateful for the first responders, members of the military, and all those who heroically rushed to provide aid and assistance on that day in the aftermath of the attacks. As we remember 9/11, especially today, we must remain steadfast in America’s commitment to care for one another in times of crisis and know that we are always stronger together. Sincerely, Steve Sisolak

clean_body: | | | | | Twenty years later our nation still holds a solemn remembrance of this day. We will never forget and will always grieve the loss of life on that terrible morning. And we remain grateful for the first responders, members of the military, and all those who heroically rushed to provide aid and assistance on that day in the aftermath of the attacks. As we remember 9/11, especially today, we must remain steadfast in America’s commitment to care for one another in times of crisis and know that we are always stronger together. name: Kevin Kiley
subject: Lawsuit against Biden
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kevin Kiley for Governor 2021 9458 Treelake Road, Granite Bay, CA 95746, United States
body:
|
---
Hi Peter, I've just published a new blog post: [Suing the President](https://kevinkiley.nationbuilder.com/r?u=Bjc8XUdeJ9yV6eR3mI9Z6HRd5urWv8u8i-Pa-jQziooHgbwlJeBzkkFAyGVssWf2vd2WhzRQ9v2lMhmK1zaVzA&e=eff202566bfe6143aa18c9c627e100fe&utm_source=kevinkiley&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=sept_11_final&n=1) As a Legislator I sued the Governor and as Governor I wouldn’t hesitate to sue the President if necessary to protect the rights of Californians. The Recall is a movement to restore constitutional government, and the stakes have just been raised. We can’t allow executive overreach at one level to be replaced by executive overreach at another. As Governor, I'd use all tools at my disposal, including lawsuits, to protect Californians from attacks on liberty from the federal government or any quarter. As to the election, I'll be voting at a local polling place Tuesday morning. Newsom cast his ballot yesterday, and as he did, he exhorted people to “vote no and go.” I have a different message: vote yes and stay. Stay involved. We all have a part to play in restoring government by the people. You can see our final campaign ads [here](https://kevinkiley.nationbuilder.com/r?u=Bjc8XUdeJ9yV6eR3mI9Z6HRd5urWv8u8i-Pa-jQzioqQGFOaJ1Zg3H3SYSMog_2DDBps26ZrabxRJhDRSuD1VA&e=eff202566bfe6143aa18c9c627e100fe&utm_source=kevinkiley&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=sept_11_final&n=2).
